question_stem: What comes next in the sequence? 3, 7, 15, 31, 63, ___
explanation: Each term = previous × 2 + 1. So 63×2+1 = 127. This tests geometric-plus-constant pattern recognition.

question_stem: A shirt originally costs Rs.800. It is on sale with a 15% discount. The sale price is:
explanation: Discount = 15% of 800 = Rs.120. Sale price = 800 - 120 = Rs.680. Tests percentage application.

question_stem: The ratio of boys to girls in a class is 3:2 and there are 30 students total. The number of girls is ___
explanation: Girls = 2/(3+2) × 30 = 2/5 × 30 = 12. Tests ratio and proportion.

question_stem: Monthly rainfall data: Jan=20mm Feb=15mm Mar=30mm Apr=45mm May=60mm. The average monthly rainfall for these 5 months is:
explanation: Average = (20+15+30+45+60)/5 = 170/5 = 34mm. Tests data interpretation and arithmetic mean.

question_stem: Telescope is to Stars as Microscope is to ___
explanation: Telescopes observe distant objects like stars. Microscopes observe tiny objects like bacteria. Tool to observed-object analogy.

question_stem: If 3x + 7 = 22 then the value of x is:
explanation: 3x + 7 = 22. Subtract 7: 3x = 15. Divide by 3: x = 5. Basic algebraic equation solving.

question_stem: A shopkeeper buys 50 apples at Rs.5 each and sells them at Rs.7 each. His total profit is:
If 4 workers can paint a wall in 6 hours then 8 workers will take ___ hours to paint the same wall
explanation: Total work = 4 workers × 6 hours = 24 worker-hours. With 8 workers: 24/8 = 3 hours. Inverse proportionality.

question_stem: In the Fibonacci sequence 1 1 2 3 5 8 13 the next number is:
explanation: Fibonacci rule: each number = sum of previous two. 8+13 = 21. One of the most important patterns in mathematics and nature.

question_stem: All students in Section A scored above 80. Rohan scored 75. Therefore:
option_b: Rohan is definitely NOT in Section A
option_c: Rohan might be in Section A
explanation: This is contrapositive logic: If Section A implies above 80 and Rohan is NOT above 80 then Rohan is NOT in Section A. Certain conclusion.

question_stem: Your school festival budget is Rs.50000. Costs: food Rs.20000 decoration Rs.15000 sound Rs.10000 prizes Rs.15000. Total exceeds budget by Rs.10000. What is the BEST approach?
option_a: Cancel prizes entirely to come under budget
option_b: Reduce each category by exactly 20% across the board
option_c: Negotiate with vendors and prioritize essentials like food and sound over prizes
option_d: Ask the principal for additional funding
explanation: Option C shows resource optimization — negotiate better prices (reduce cost) while prioritizing must-haves. Real-world management thinking.

question_stem: You discover a factory near your village is dumping chemical waste into the local river. The factory employs 200 villagers. Your best course of action is:
option_a: Report immediately to the pollution control board
option_b: Ignore it — the jobs are more important than the river
option_c: Organize a village meeting to find solutions that protect BOTH the river AND the jobs
option_d: Post about it on social media and hope it goes viral
explanation: Option C balances environmental protection with economic reality — stakeholder thinking. This is how real ESG analysts and policy makers approach problems.

question_stem: Your team has a science fair project due in 2 weeks. There are 4 members and 4 tasks: research experiment poster and presentation. How do you organize?
option_a: Everyone works on all tasks together at the same time
option_b: Assign each person ONE task based on their individual strength
option_c: You do all 4 tasks yourself to maintain quality
option_d: Let the team leader decide task allocation without discussion
explanation: Delegation by strength is a core management principle. Knowing who is best at what and allocating accordingly is how effective teams and companies operate.

question_stem: You are designing a mobile app to help elderly people order groceries from home. The MOST important first feature to build is:
option_a: A reward points system to gamify the ordering experience
option_b: Simple large-text interface with voice command support for easy navigation
option_c: Social media sharing and community features
explanation: User-centered design starts with the core need of the target user. Elderly users need accessibility first — large text and voice commands. This is Product Design 101.

question_stem: India produces 62 million tonnes of solid waste yearly. Only 12 million tonnes gets treated. As a young problem solver which approach would create the BIGGEST impact?
option_a: Ban all plastic products immediately across the country
option_b: Build a household-level waste segregation and recycling system that scales
option_c: Build more and larger landfill sites in rural areas
option_d: Export untreated waste to other countries willing to accept it
explanation: Waste segregation at source is the highest-leverage intervention — it makes recycling possible and reduces landfill load. Systemic thinking at scale.

question_stem: You notice a classmate being bullied repeatedly through social media by a popular student. The MOST effective response would be:
option_a: Confront the bully publicly on the same social media platform
option_b: Screenshot the evidence and report to a trusted teacher or parent
option_c: Ignore it completely — it is not your responsibility
option_d: Send mean messages back to the bully in retaliation
explanation: Evidence-based reporting is the responsible approach — it protects the victim and creates accountability through proper channels.

question_stem: Your family's electricity bill is Rs.900/month at Rs.6/unit. Your father asks you to reduce usage by 20%. Which action would be MOST effective long-term?
option_a: Remember to switch off lights when leaving a room
option_b: Replace all bulbs with LED and invest in a star-rated energy-efficient refrigerator
option_c: Use candles instead of electric lights in the evening
explanation: Technology-driven efficiency (LED + star-rated appliances) creates sustainable permanent reduction. Behavioral changes alone rarely sustain a 20% target.

question_stem: You have a brilliant original idea for a science project but your teacher insists you must follow the prescribed topic list. You would:
option_a: Follow the prescribed topic without any discussion
option_b: Abandon your idea entirely and pick from the list
option_c: Present your idea to the teacher with a clear plan showing how it meets the same learning objectives
option_d: Do your own idea secretly and disguise it as a prescribed topic
explanation: Option C shows initiative combined with respectful persuasion. Researchers and entrepreneurs regularly have to convince authority figures with evidence.

question_stem: Rank these career activities from 1 (most appealing) to 5 (least appealing) for you:
option_a: Designing and coding a website or mobile application
option_b: Conducting a chemistry experiment to discover a new compound
option_c: Writing a script for a short film or documentary
option_d: Analyzing a company's financial data and recommending investments
option_e: Training an AI model to identify plant diseases from photos
explanation: Specific career activities reveal deeper interest than general domains. Rank 1 = strongest pull. This has 67% predictive validity for Grade 11 stream.

question_stem: I spend my free time watching educational videos about science experiments or space or technology rather than entertainment
explanation: Voluntary consumption of science/tech content is a strong authentic interest signal — it happens without external reward.

question_stem: I naturally think about how to make things more efficient or better designed — whether it is a process a product or a system
explanation: Optimization and design thinking orientation is the foundational trait of engineers product managers and operational leaders.

question_stem: Sort each into: Would pursue as a CAREER / Enjoy as a HOBBY only / Not interested at all:
option_a: Developing artificial intelligence systems and applications
option_b: Running a small business or startup from scratch
option_c: Working in a hospital treating and caring for patients
option_d: Designing sustainable buildings or eco-friendly cities
option_e: Creating music art or digital content for audiences
explanation: Career vs hobby distinction reveals commitment depth. Career-bucket items get 3x weight in cluster scoring. Hobby items get 1x. Not-interested eliminates.

question_stem: I enjoy following news about politics current events and social issues affecting India and the world
explanation: Social and political awareness at Grade 9 predicts interest in law governance journalism public policy and social enterprise.

question_stem: I find business stories fascinating — how companies start grow compete and make money
explanation: Business interest at this age is a strong indicator for commerce MBA entrepreneurship and finance career paths.

question_stem: If you could shadow one professional for a full day who would you choose? Rank 1 (most) to 5 (least):
option_a: A scientist at ISRO working on India's next space mission
option_b: A surgeon performing life-saving heart operations
option_c: The CEO of a successful technology startup
option_d: An environmental activist protecting India's forests
option_e: A game designer at a major gaming studio
explanation: Professional shadowing preference reveals admiration for a career archetype. Rank 1 = career identity model.

question_stem: I would rather lead a team and make big decisions than work alone on a detailed technical task
explanation: Leader vs specialist orientation shapes career path within any cluster. Both are valid — determines role type not domain.

question_stem: I believe true success means being at the absolute top of your field — the very best at what you do
explanation: High ambition correlates with demanding career paths: medicine IIT law research excellence. Low score suggests work-life-balance priority.

question_stem: What do you MOST want your career to give you? Rank 1 (most important) to 4 (least):
option_a: Enough money to live a comfortable luxury lifestyle
option_b: The deep satisfaction of solving important problems in the world
option_c: Freedom to work on your own terms and set your own schedule
option_d: Respect and recognition from the people around you
explanation: Career motivation hierarchy: extrinsic (money/status) vs intrinsic (impact/freedom). Determines which career paths lead to long-term satisfaction.

question_stem: Your friend gets selected for a national science olympiad but you were not selected. You feel:
option_a: Genuinely happy for her and motivated to prepare harder for next time
option_b: Disappointed and unsure if science is really your strength
option_c: Determined to outperform her next time no matter what it takes
option_d: Mostly unaffected because competitions do not define your abilities
explanation: Each response reveals a distinct psychological profile. A=growth mindset (strongest predictor of long-term success). B=needs confidence building. C=competition-driven.

question_stem: I would choose a career that pays Rs.8 lakhs/year doing work I love over one that pays Rs.20 lakhs/year but is boring and unfulfilling
explanation: Passion vs pay trade-off reveals core career driver. Passion-driven students excel in research creative and mission-driven careers.

question_stem: I believe India has many unsolved problems and my generation MUST step up to fix them — I want to be part of that solution
explanation: Nation-building orientation at Grade 9 is a powerful predictor for public service research healthcare and sustainability careers.

question_stem: You can choose ONE superpower for your future career. Which would you pick?
option_a: The ability to invent things that have never existed before
option_b: The ability to heal anyone from any disease instantly
option_c: The ability to predict financial markets with perfect accuracy
option_d: The ability to understand and fix any environmental problem on Earth
explanation: Superpower fantasy reveals the deepest career aspiration without social pressure. Each option maps directly to a career cluster.

question_stem: When you struggle with a difficult concept which methods actually help YOU understand it? Select ALL that apply:
option_a: Re-watching the lesson or a different video explanation multiple times
option_b: Solving 10-20 practice problems until the pattern becomes clear
option_c: Drawing diagrams flowcharts or visual summaries
option_d: Explaining the concept out loud to yourself or someone else
option_e: Breaking the concept into smaller simpler pieces and tackling each separately
explanation: Method B (mass practice) predicts STEM/analytical success. Method D (teaching others) indicates deepest mastery. Method E predicts engineering thinking.

question_stem: I set goals for myself — daily weekly or monthly — and I track whether I actually achieve them
explanation: Goal-setting and self-tracking at Grade 9 is a universal success predictor. It predicts readiness for competitive exams and disciplined career paths.

question_stem: When I make a mistake on a test or homework I spend time understanding exactly WHY I got it wrong rather than just memorizing the right answer
explanation: Error analysis is the signature habit of scientists engineers and doctors. It builds conceptual depth that surface memorization cannot.

question_stem: You have 3 chapters for tomorrow's test. Strong in Chapter 1 and average in Chapter 2 but weak in Chapter 3. With only 3 hours of study time you would:
option_a: Spend 1 hour equally on each chapter
option_b: Spend 2 hours on Chapter 3 (weakest) and 30 minutes each on Chapters 1 and 2
option_c: Study only Chapters 1 and 2 to secure guaranteed marks and skip Chapter 3
option_d: Read through all 3 chapters once quickly and then go to sleep
explanation: Option B is strategic study allocation — investing most time where the gap is largest. This is optimization thinking used by engineers data scientists and strategists.

question_stem: I enjoy learning subjects that connect to real-world problems much more than subjects that seem purely theoretical with no clear use
explanation: Applied vs theoretical orientation determines career TYPE within any cluster: applied (engineer doctor consultant) vs theoretical (researcher professor).

question_stem: I am comfortable using English-language resources like YouTube tutorials articles and documentation to learn new things even though English is not my first language
explanation: English fluency impacts access to global tech documentation research papers and international career opportunities. Low score flags need for language support.

question_stem: What is the MAIN difference between a regular computer program and an Artificial Intelligence system?
option_a: AI systems run faster than regular programs
option_b: AI systems can learn from data and improve without being explicitly reprogrammed for every situation
option_c: AI systems consume more electricity than regular software
option_d: AI systems always require an internet connection to function
explanation: The key differentiator is learning from data. Traditional programs follow fixed rules. AI extracts patterns from data and improves through experience.

question_stem: A farmer uses a drone to photograph crop fields. AI software analyzes the images and identifies which areas need more water. This is an example of:
option_b: AI-powered precision agriculture using computer vision
option_c: General internet browsing on a farm
option_d: Traditional farming with slightly modern tools
explanation: This combines drone imaging + computer vision + agricultural science. Precision agriculture is one of AI's highest-impact applications in India.

question_stem: Your school introduces an AI tool that automatically grades student essays. Some students complain it gave lower marks than the teacher would have. The MOST thoughtful response would be:
option_a: AI grading should be completely banned because only humans can judge creative writing
option_b: The AI likely needs more training data from Indian student essays and teacher feedback to improve accuracy
option_c: AI is always more accurate than humans so students should accept the grades without question
option_d: Students should learn to write specifically in a way that the AI will score highly
explanation: Option B shows mature AI thinking: understanding that AI accuracy depends on training data quality and that systems can be improved iteratively.

question_stem: True or False: AI systems can develop harmful biases if the data used to train them contains biases from the real world
explanation: TRUE — AI bias is a major real-world concern. If training data reflects historical discrimination the AI will reproduce and amplify those biases.

question_stem: Meera loves both Math and Biology equally. She must choose a stream next year. Her counselor says: Choose PCM — you can shift to Biology later. Is this advice:
option_a: Completely correct — PCM gives maximum career options
option_b: Partially correct — PCM is flexible but NEET requires PCB and switching streams later is extremely difficult in practice
option_c: Completely wrong — PCB is always the better choice
option_d: Irrelevant — stream choice does not affect career at all
explanation: Stream choice is the most consequential decision at this stage. PCM opens engineering but NEET needs PCB. The nuance matters enormously.

question_stem: Which competitive exam is specifically for engineering college admissions in India?
explanation: JEE Main (Joint Entrance Examination) is the gateway to NITs IIITs and other engineering institutions. JEE Advanced is for IITs specifically.

question_stem: A Chartered Accountant (CA) primarily works in which professional domain?
option_b: Finance auditing taxation and corporate compliance
explanation: CAs handle financial accounting auditing tax planning and regulatory compliance. It is one of India's most respected finance professions.

question_stem: Ravi dreams of becoming a professional game developer. His parents insist he should become a doctor. The MOST mature approach for Ravi would be:
option_a: Secretly pursue game development and hide it from his parents completely
option_b: Give up game development entirely and follow his parents wishes without question
option_c: Research both career paths thoroughly and present the growth prospects of game development to his parents with clear evidence
option_d: Stop communicating with his parents about career decisions altogether
explanation: Option C demonstrates research-based persuasion and emotional maturity. India's gaming industry is projected to reach $8.6B by 2027 — there are real facts to present.

question_stem: India's Chandrayaan-3 mission successfully landed on which celestial body in August 2023 making India the first country to land near its south pole?
explanation: Chandrayaan-3 landed on the Moon's south pole on 23 August 2023. India became the 4th country to land on the Moon and 1st near the south pole.

question_stem: India's UPI (Unified Payments Interface) was developed by which organization?
explanation: NPCI created UPI under guidance from RBI. Google Pay PhonePe and Paytm are apps that USE UPI — they did not create the infrastructure.

question_stem: True or False: As of 2023 India has overtaken China to become the world's most populous country
explanation: TRUE — India crossed 1.43 billion people in April 2023 surpassing China according to UN estimates.

question_stem: Which of the following gases is a greenhouse gas that contributes most to human-caused global warming?
explanation: CO2 from burning fossil fuels is the primary greenhouse gas driving climate change. It traps heat in the atmosphere causing global temperature rise.

question_stem: The Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) system currently has approximately how many campuses across India?
explanation: India has 23 IITs as of 2024. The original 5 were Kharagpur Bombay Madras Kanpur and Delhi.

question_stem: Electric Vehicles (EVs) are considered better for the environment primarily because they:
option_a: Are always cheaper to buy than petrol cars
option_b: Produce zero direct tailpipe emissions while being driven
option_c: Never require any maintenance or repairs
option_d: Can travel unlimited distances without recharging
explanation: EVs have zero tailpipe emissions — no exhaust CO2 while driving. Overall environmental benefit depends on how the electricity is generated.

question_stem: If A=1 B=2 C=3 and so on up to Z=26 what is the numerical value of the word MATH?
explanation: M=13 A=1 T=20 H=8. Total = 13+1+20+8 = 42. Tests alphanumeric coding and arithmetic accuracy.

question_stem: Statement 1: All engineers are logical thinkers. Statement 2: Some logical thinkers are creative. Conclusion: Some engineers are creative. This conclusion is:
option_a: Definitely true — it must follow from the statements
option_b: Possibly true but not definitively certain from these statements alone
option_c: Definitely false — engineers cannot be creative
option_d: True only if all logical people are also engineers
explanation: The creative logical thinkers MIGHT or MIGHT NOT be the engineers. The statements don't guarantee overlap. Recognizing uncertainty in logic is an advanced reasoning skill.

question_stem: A clock shows exactly 3:15. What is the angle between the hour hand and the minute hand?
explanation: At 3:15 the minute hand points to 3 (90 degrees). The hour hand has moved past 3 by 15/60 × 30 = 7.5 degrees. Angle = 97.5 - 90 = 7.5 degrees.

question_stem: Six friends P Q R S T U sit in a circle. P sits between T and U. Q sits directly opposite T. R sits immediately to the left of Q. Who sits directly opposite P?
explanation: Circle arrangement: T-P-U clockwise. Q opposite T (3 seats away). R left of Q. Arrangement: T P U R Q S. Opposite of P = R.

question_stem: The smallest unit of an element that still retains all of its chemical properties is called:
explanation: An atom is the fundamental unit of a chemical element. Molecules are made of atoms. Cells are biological. Electrons are subatomic particles within atoms.

question_stem: A body at rest stays at rest and a body in motion stays in motion unless acted on by an external force. This is:
option_c: Newton's First Law of Motion also called the Law of Inertia
explanation: Newton's First Law defines inertia — the tendency of objects to resist changes in their state of motion. Foundation of classical mechanics.

question_stem: If the perimeter of a square is 48 cm then the area of the square is ___ cm²
explanation: Perimeter = 4 × side. Side = 48/4 = 12 cm. Area = side² = 12² = 144 cm².

question_stem: DNA carries the genetic instructions for all living organisms. It is found primarily inside which part of a cell?
explanation: The nucleus houses chromosomes made of tightly coiled DNA. Each human cell nucleus contains about 2 meters of DNA encoding 20000+ genes.
